THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT HAS NOT NEGATED PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS IN DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS WHILE REPAIRING THE PRODUCTS. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE ISSUE OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION AS NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SPACE SYSTEMS/LORAL, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Appearances:
Christine Renken of Clapper Patti Schweizer & Mason (440.4262) for plaintiff and responding party
D. Paul Bird of McKenna Long & Aldridge (267.4075) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication issues one to four is based on alleged insufficient evidence of exposure to asbestos in products manufactured or supplied by defendant. The motion is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant has not negated plaintiff's deposition testimony of exposure to asbestos in defendant's products while repairing the products. (See e.g. plaintiff's deposition 16, 18, 21, 56, and 343.) Defendant's challenge the persuasiveness of the testimony but that is not sufficient to shift the burden of proof. The Combs declaration is insufficient to establish that defendant never made such products; at most, it creates a triable issue of fact. The motion for summary adjudication on the issue of punitive damages is granted. Summary adjudication is granted as to the 3rd cause of action as no opposition was filed. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780
